description: Fast, versatile file-copying tool using delta-transfer for local and remote sync.

arguments.name: Archive Mode
arguments.description: Archive mode: equals -rlptgoD. Preserves recursion, symlinks, permissions, times, group, owner, and device files.

arguments.name: Dry Run
arguments.description: Perform a trial run making no changes. Shows what would have been transferred.

arguments.name: Verbose
arguments.description: Increase verbosity. Can be repeated (e.g. -vv) for more detail.

arguments.name: Quiet
arguments.description: Suppress non-error messages. Useful when invoking rsync from cron.

arguments.name: Suppress MOTD
arguments.description: Suppress message-of-the-day output from daemon at start of transfer.

arguments.name: Human Readable
arguments.description: Output numbers in a human-readable format (e.g. 1.23K, 4.56M).

arguments.name: Recursive
arguments.description: Recurse into directories. Implied by --archive.

arguments.name: Relative Paths
arguments.description: Use relative path names, sending the full path to the server rather than just the last component.

arguments.name: No Implied Dirs
arguments.description: With --relative, do not include attributes of implied directories from source names in the transfer.

arguments.name: Dirs (No Recurse)
arguments.description: Transfer directories without recursing into their contents.

arguments.name: Prune Empty Dirs
arguments.description: Prune empty directory chains from the file-list.

arguments.name: Copy Symlinks
arguments.description: When symlinks are encountered, recreate the symlink on the destination.

arguments.name: Dereference Symlinks
arguments.description: Copy the referent (target) of symlinks rather than the symlink itself.

arguments.name: Copy Unsafe Symlinks
arguments.description: Copy referents of symlinks that point outside the source tree.

arguments.name: Safe Symlinks
arguments.description: Ignore symlinks that point outside the destination tree.

arguments.name: Munge Symlinks
arguments.description: Munge symlinks on the receiving side to make them unusable but recoverable (security feature).

arguments.name: Copy Dir Symlinks (Sender)
arguments.description: Treat symlinks to directories on the sending side as real directories.

arguments.name: Keep Dir Symlinks (Receiver)
arguments.description: Treat symlinks to directories on the receiving side as real directories if they match a real directory from the sender.

arguments.name: Preserve Hard Links
arguments.description: Recreate hard links on the destination to match the source.

arguments.name: Preserve Permissions
arguments.description: Preserve source file permissions on the destination.

arguments.name: Preserve Executability
arguments.description: Preserve executability of regular files when --perms is not enabled.

arguments.name: Preserve ACLs
arguments.description: Update destination ACLs to match source. Implies --perms.

arguments.name: Preserve Extended Attributes
arguments.description: Update destination extended attributes (xattrs) to match source.

arguments.name: Chmod
arguments.description: Apply chmod mode(s) to permissions of transferred files. Prefix D for dirs-only, F for files-only.

arguments.name: Preserve Owner
arguments.description: Preserve owner of destination file. Only effective when run as super-user.

arguments.name: Preserve Group
arguments.description: Preserve group of destination file.

arguments.name: Preserve Devices
arguments.description: Preserve character and block device files. Requires super-user on receiver.

arguments.name: Preserve Specials
arguments.description: Preserve special files such as named sockets and fifos.

arguments.name: Preserve Times
arguments.description: Preserve modification times. Without this, all files will be re-examined on the next transfer.

arguments.name: Omit Dir Times
arguments.description: Omit directories when preserving modification times. Useful with NFS on the receiving side.

arguments.name: Omit Symlink Times
arguments.description: Omit symlinks when preserving modification times.

arguments.name: Super
arguments.description: Tell the receiving side to attempt super-user activities (owner, group, devices) even if not running as super-user.

arguments.name: Fake Super
arguments.description: Simulate super-user by saving privileged attributes (owner, group, device info, permissions) via extended attributes.

arguments.name: Numeric IDs
arguments.description: Transfer numeric UID/GID values rather than mapping by user/group name.

arguments.name: User Map
arguments.description: Custom username mapping for transferred files (e.g. user1:user2,user3:user4).

arguments.name: Group Map
arguments.description: Custom groupname mapping for transferred files (e.g. group1:group2).

arguments.name: Chown
arguments.description: Simple username:groupname mapping applied to all transferred files.

arguments.name: Checksum
arguments.description: Skip files based on checksum rather than mod-time and size. Slower but more accurate.

arguments.name: Ignore Times
arguments.description: Disable the quick-check behavior — do not skip files that match size and timestamp.

arguments.name: Size Only
arguments.description: Skip files that match in size, ignoring timestamps. Useful after migrating from non-timestamp-preserving mirrors.

arguments.name: Modify Window
arguments.description: Set timestamp comparison window in seconds (default 0). Use 1 for FAT filesystems. Negative values enable nanosecond comparison (requires rsync 3.1.3+).

arguments.name: Checksum Choice
arguments.description: Override the checksum algorithm(s). Accepts one or two comma-separated names: auto, md4, md5, none. Requires rsync 3.x.

arguments.name: Whole File
arguments.description: Disable delta-transfer algorithm — send whole files. Default for local copies.

arguments.name: No Whole File
arguments.description: Turn off --whole-file, enabling delta-transfer. Use to override the local-copy default.

arguments.name: One Filesystem
arguments.description: Avoid crossing filesystem boundaries when recursing.

arguments.name: In-Place Updates
arguments.description: Write updated file data directly to the destination file instead of creating a new copy. WARNING: file is in an inconsistent state during transfer.

arguments.name: Append Data
arguments.description: Update files by appending data to the end. Presumes existing destination data is identical to the start of the source file. Implies --inplace. DANGEROUS: only use if you are certain files grow only by appending.

arguments.name: Append With Verify
arguments.description: Like --append but includes existing destination data in the full-file checksum verification step.

arguments.name: Sparse Files
arguments.description: Handle sparse files efficiently so they take up less space on the destination.

arguments.name: Preallocate
arguments.description: Preallocate each destination file to its eventual size before writing. Uses fallocate(2) on Linux.

arguments.name: Update (Skip Newer)
arguments.description: Skip destination files that are newer than the source.

arguments.name: Existing Only
arguments.description: Skip creating files that do not yet exist on the destination (alias: --ignore-non-existing).

arguments.name: Ignore Existing
arguments.description: Skip updating files that already exist on the destination.

arguments.name: Remove Source Files
arguments.description: Remove non-directory source files after successful transfer.

arguments.name: Delay Updates
arguments.description: Place all updated files into a holding directory and then rename them into place atomically at the end of the transfer.

arguments.name: Fuzzy Basis
arguments.description: Look for a similar file on the destination to use as a basis when no exact match exists, saving bandwidth.

arguments.name: Protect Args
arguments.description: Protect filenames containing spaces or special characters from shell interpretation on the remote side.

arguments.name: Block Size
arguments.description: Override the block size used in rsync's delta-transfer algorithm.

arguments.name: Filter Rule
arguments.description: Add a file-filtering rule. Rules are applied in order; use +/- prefixes for include/exclude.

arguments.name: CVS Exclude
arguments.description: Auto-ignore files CVS would ignore: *.o, *.bak, .git/, .svn/, core, etc.

arguments.name: Exclude Pattern
arguments.description: Exclude files matching PATTERN from the transfer.

arguments.name: Exclude From File
arguments.description: Read exclude patterns from FILE, one per line.

arguments.name: Include Pattern
arguments.description: Do not exclude files matching PATTERN. Used with --exclude to build complex rules.

arguments.name: Include From File
arguments.description: Read include patterns from FILE, one per line.

arguments.name: Files From
arguments.description: Read list of source filenames to transfer from FILE.

arguments.name: Null-Delimited Lists
arguments.description: All filenames from --files-from, --exclude-from, and --include-from are delimited by null characters instead of newlines.

arguments.name: Max File Size
arguments.description: Skip transferring files larger than SIZE. Accepts suffixes: K, M, G (1024-based) or KB, MB, GB (1000-based).

arguments.name: Min File Size
arguments.description: Skip transferring files smaller than SIZE. Accepts same suffixes as --max-size.

arguments.name: Delete Extraneous
arguments.description: Delete files on the receiver that do not exist on the sender. Run with --dry-run first to preview deletions.

arguments.name: Delete Before Transfer
arguments.description: Perform deletions on receiver before transfer starts. Implies --delete.

arguments.name: Delete During Transfer
arguments.description: Delete on receiver incrementally as the transfer happens (default for rsync 3.0+). Alias: --del. Implies --delete.

arguments.name: Delete Delay
arguments.description: Compute deletions during transfer but execute them after the transfer completes. Implies --delete.

arguments.name: Delete After Transfer
arguments.description: Perform deletions on receiver after transfer completes. Useful when sending new per-directory merge files. Implies --delete.

arguments.name: Delete Excluded
arguments.description: Also delete files on the receiver that match exclude patterns.

arguments.name: Ignore Missing Args
arguments.description: Suppress errors for explicitly requested source files that cannot be found.

arguments.name: Delete Missing Args
arguments.description: Delete corresponding destination files for source args that cannot be found. Implies --ignore-missing-args.

arguments.name: Ignore IO Errors
arguments.description: Proceed with deletions even when IO errors are encountered on the sending side.

arguments.name: Force Directory Deletion
arguments.description: Allow deletion of non-empty directories when they are to be replaced by non-directories.

arguments.name: Max Delete
arguments.description: Do not delete more than NUM files or directories. Use 0 to warn without deleting.

arguments.name: Backup
arguments.description: Rename pre-existing destination files with a suffix before overwriting (default suffix: ~).

arguments.name: Backup Directory
arguments.description: Store all backup files in DIR instead of alongside the original. Use absolute paths or paths starting with '../'.

arguments.name: Backup Suffix
arguments.description: Override the default backup suffix (default: ~ when no --backup-dir, empty string otherwise).

arguments.name: Temp Directory
arguments.description: Use DIR as a scratch directory when creating temporary copies of transferred files on the receiving side.

arguments.name: Compare Dest
arguments.description: Use DIR on the destination as an additional reference directory for comparisons. Files unchanged relative to DIR are not transferred.

arguments.name: Copy Dest
arguments.description: Like --compare-dest but also copies unchanged files from DIR using local hard-links.

arguments.name: Link Dest
arguments.description: Hard-link unchanged files from DIR into the destination rather than transferring them. Enables space-efficient incremental backups.

arguments.name: Keep Partial Files
arguments.description: Keep partially transferred files on the receiver if the transfer is interrupted (speeds up subsequent retries).

arguments.name: Partial Dir
arguments.description: Store partially transferred files in DIR instead of in the destination directory.

arguments.name: Progress
arguments.description: Show per-file progress during transfer (speed, percentage, estimated time remaining).

arguments.name: Statistics
arguments.description: Print a verbose set of transfer statistics at the end.

arguments.name: Remote Shell
arguments.description: Specify the remote shell command (default: ssh). Accepts arguments, e.g. 'ssh -p 2222'.

arguments.name: Remote rsync Path
arguments.description: Path to the rsync executable on the remote machine.

arguments.name: Remote Option
arguments.description: Send OPTION to the remote rsync only. Useful to limit effects to one side of the transfer.

arguments.name: Compress
arguments.description: Compress file data during transfer. Useful on slow links.

arguments.name: Compress Level
arguments.description: Explicitly set the compression level (0 = disable, higher = more compression). Requires rsync 3.x.

arguments.name: Skip Compress
arguments.description: Comma-separated list of file suffixes that should not be compressed (already compressed formats). Requires rsync 3.x.

arguments.name: Bandwidth Limit
arguments.description: Limit I/O bandwidth. Accepts a value in KiB/s or with suffixes (K, M, G). Use 0 for no limit.

arguments.name: Port
arguments.description: Specify alternate TCP port for daemon connections (default: 873). Only for double-colon (::) syntax.

arguments.name: Bind Address
arguments.description: Bind to the specified IP address or hostname for outgoing connections.

arguments.name: Socket Options
arguments.description: Specify custom TCP socket options for direct daemon connections. See setsockopt(2) for available options.

arguments.name: Blocking I/O
arguments.description: Use blocking I/O when launching a remote shell transport. May be needed for some remote shells.

arguments.name: No Blocking I/O
arguments.description: Turn off --blocking-io when it is the default.

arguments.name: I/O Timeout
arguments.description: Set maximum I/O timeout in seconds. rsync exits if no data is transferred within this window. Default 0 (no timeout).

arguments.name: Connection Timeout
arguments.description: Set timeout in seconds for the initial connection to an rsync daemon.

arguments.name: Prefer IPv4
arguments.description: Prefer IPv4 when creating sockets.

arguments.name: Prefer IPv6
arguments.description: Prefer IPv6 when creating sockets.

arguments.name: Itemize Changes
arguments.description: Output a change-summary string for every updated file (11-character code indicating what changed).

arguments.name: Output Format
arguments.description: Control per-file output format during transfer. Uses printf-like format codes (e.g. %n = filename, %l = length).

arguments.name: Log Format
arguments.description: Older alias for --out-format. Specify per-file log format.

arguments.name: Log File
arguments.description: Write transfer log to FILE. Each line records a transferred file.

arguments.name: Log File Format
arguments.description: Specify the format of entries written to --log-file. Uses same format codes as --out-format.

arguments.name: List Only
arguments.description: List files without transferring them. Equivalent to omitting the destination.

arguments.name: 8-Bit Output
arguments.description: Leave high-bit characters unescaped in output rather than showing them as backslash-escaped sequences.

arguments.name: Info Flags
arguments.description: Fine-grained control over informational output. Use --info=help to see flag names. Added in rsync 3.1.0.

arguments.name: Debug Flags
arguments.description: Fine-grained control over debug output. Use --debug=help to see flag names. Added in rsync 3.1.0.

arguments.name: Messages to stderr
arguments.description: Send all rsync output directly to STDERR rather than through the protocol. Mainly for debugging.

arguments.name: Charset Conversion
arguments.description: Request charset conversion of filenames. Format: FROM,TO (e.g. UTF-8,LATIN-1).

arguments.name: Write Batch
arguments.description: Write a batch update to FILE. The batch can later be replayed on identical systems using --read-batch.

arguments.name: Write Batch Only
arguments.description: Like --write-batch but do not perform the actual transfer — only write the batch file.

arguments.name: Read Batch
arguments.description: Apply a previously generated batch update from FILE.

arguments.name: Checksum Seed
arguments.description: Set an explicit block/file checksum seed. Mainly for batch mode reproducibility.

arguments.name: Daemon Mode
arguments.description: Run rsync as a daemon accepting connections via TCP (port 873) or via remote shell.

arguments.name: No Detach
arguments.description: Do not detach from the parent process when running as a daemon. Required for Cygwin services and useful under daemontools.

arguments.name: Config File
arguments.description: Specify an alternate rsyncd.conf configuration file (default: /etc/rsyncd.conf). Only relevant with --daemon.

arguments.name: Password File
arguments.description: Read daemon authentication password from FILE. File must not be world-readable.

arguments.name: Help
arguments.description: Print a short help page describing available options and exit.

arguments.name: Version
arguments.description: Print the rsync version number and exit.

arguments.name: Source
arguments.description: Source path. Use [USER@]HOST:PATH for remote shell, [USER@]HOST::MODULE/PATH or rsync://[USER@]HOST/MODULE/PATH for daemon.

arguments.name: Destination
arguments.description: Destination path. Use [USER@]HOST:PATH for remote shell, [USER@]HOST::MODULE/PATH for daemon.
